articles.title: YOUR AI IS ONLY AS GOOD AS THE PROMPT YOU FEED IT.
articles.summary: Wispr Flow [5] lets you speak detailed prompts instead of typing short ones. Explain the full context, the constraints, the edge cases, in the time it takes to type a sentence. 4x faster than typing.

articles.title: Try Wispr Flow Free → [6] | Download Flow
KIMI K3
articles.summary: Moonshot has launched Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter multimodal model with a 1-million-token context window and optimizations for faster long-context decoding and agentic coding. The model is available through Kimi's products and API, with open weights scheduled for release on July 27.

articles.title: GEMINI 3.5 PRO REPORTEDLY FACED DELAYS
articles.summary: Alphabet shares fell 4% after a report said Google had delayed Gemini 3.5 Pro while working to improve its performance, particularly for coding. Google said the model remained in partner testing alongside an upgraded Flash model and other releases.

articles.title: INTRODUCING LM STUDIO BIONIC: THE AI AGENT FOR OPEN MODELS
articles.summary: Bionic is an AI agent for coding, research, and document management with open models, offering local and cloud options for privacy and cost control. It supports offline voice transcription with Voxtral by Mistral AI and excels at coding by inspecting codebases and solving issues with models like GLM 5.2. Bionic enhances productivity by working with various documents while ensuring security with sandbox environments and native web search integration.

articles.title: HARNESS HANDBOOK TO MAP AGENT BEHAVIOR TO CODE
articles.summary: Harness Handbook is a behavior-level map for coding-agent harnesses, connecting plain-language questions about execution, permissions, and safety to the exact prompts, tools, state logic, configuration, and telemetry that implement them.

articles.title: HOW ANTHROPIC RUNS LARGE-SCALE CODE MIGRATIONS WITH CLAUDE CODE
articles.summary: Anthropic uses a six-step process with Claude Code for large-scale code migrations, focusing on creating a rulebook, analyzing dependencies, and stress-testing translation rules. It deploys multiple agents to translate, review, and fix code iteratively, and emphasizes using adversarial reviewers and mechanical verification for efficient migration.

articles.title: CHOOSING GPT-5.6 SOL, TERRA, OR LUNA IN CODEX
articles.summary: GPT-5.6 splits Codex work across Sol for ambiguous, high-value problems, Terra for everyday implementation, and Luna for fast, bounded tasks. Sol Ultra adds deeper reasoning and multi-agent coordination, while clear prompts should specify goals, context, boundaries, and completion checks.

articles.title: WHEN STATE MACHINES BREAK DOWN: HANDLING WORKFLOW FAILURES IN DISTRIBUTED SYSTEMS
articles.summary: Most failure-handling logic is complex, fragile, and hard to test. This Temporal white paper [14] shows you 2 distinct patterns to take component failure in stride without constraining application structure. Learn how to implement these patterns using the same open

articles.title: source tools used at OpenAI, Lovable, and Replit. Get your copy
SCHEMA
articles.summary: Schema is a harness that makes frontier models play like physicists. It can help frontier models achieve 99% on ARC-AGI-3 Public. ARC‑AGI‑3 gives agents a game environment without an explanation of what it is seeing. Schema makes agents write each game's mechanism as an executable program, test it against reality, and plan inside it. It controls how observations are turned into a working model of a game, how predictions are tested against the interaction history, and how plans are executed and revised.

articles.title: NVIDIA NEMOTRON 3 EMBED
articles.summary: NVIDIA released three open embedding models for RAG, agentic retrieval, code search, and memory, led by an 8B model that ranked first overall on RTEB.

articles.title: COPILOT SDK
articles.summary: Embeds GitHub Copilot agents into custom applications and developer tools.

articles.title: THE BEST MODEL ROUTING IS TASK SPECIFIC
articles.summary: Model routing is a hot topic because nobody wants to pay frontier prices for every token. The best routing is deeply task-specific. The more narrowly you focus on a single workflow, the more alpha there is to exploit in terms of both accuracy and cost. The problem is figuring out which model clears the bar for which task, subject to cost/latency constraints.

articles.title: THE SELF-DRIVING COMPANY
articles.summary: Replit engineers have tripled code output without sacrificing quality, thanks to an innovative system of AI agents. These agents handle tasks like reviewing pull requests, investigating incidents, and analyzing data, enabling engineers to focus on strategic initiatives.

articles.title: NVIDIA-BACKED FIREWORKS HITS $17.5 BILLION VALUATION AS COMPANIES PURSUE CHEAPER AI MODELS
articles.summary: Nvidia-backed Fireworks secured a $17.5 billion valuation, driven by demand for cheaper AI models as companies explore open-source options.

articles.title: CONNECTED APPS IN GOOGLE AI MODE
articles.summary: Google added integrations with Instacart, Canva, and YouTube to AI Mode, allowing users to complete tasks across supported apps from its conversational search experience.

articles.title: RAMP TARGETS AI'S FASTEST-GROWING COST WITH EXPANDED TOKEN SPEND TRACKING
articles.summary: Ramp has expanded its AI Token Spend Management product to give finance teams clear insights into AI spending across providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google's Gemini.

articles.title: NOTEBOOKLM IS NOW GEMINI NOTEBOOK
articles.summary: Google has rebranded NotebookLM as Gemini Notebook, expanding its integration across the Google ecosystem, including the Gemini app and Google Search.
